_note: Curated, well-documented OFFICIAL government UAP cases from outside the US PURSUE release. Each links to an official archive or authoritative documentation. Coordinates are representative. Distinct 'International' layer in uap-pulse.

cases.title: Operação Prato — Colares
cases.description: Brazilian Air Force (FAB) field operation investigating intense UAP activity over Colares island, Pará (1977). ~4 months of reports, photos and films; archived in the Arquivo Nacional.

cases.title: A Noite Oficial dos UFOs
cases.description: On 19 May 1986, ~21 objects were tracked over São Paulo, Rio, Minas Gerais and Goiás; the FAB scrambled Mirage III and F-5 jets. The Minister of Aeronautics confirmed the events in a press conference — the 'Official Night of UFOs'.

cases.title: Varginha incident
cases.description: Widely reported 1996 case in Varginha involving alleged sightings and military activity; Brazil's most famous UAP case, debated in the press and Congress.

cases.title: Trans-en-Provence case
cases.description: France's official UAP body (GEPAN/GEIPAN, under the CNES space agency) investigated a physical-trace landing case — soil and plant alterations analyzed by state laboratories.

cases.title: Valensole case
cases.description: Farmer Maurice Masse reported a landed craft and occupants; investigated by the gendarmerie and later catalogued by GEIPAN.

cases.title: Rendlesham Forest
cases.description: 'Britain's Roswell' — USAF personnel at RAF Woodbridge/Bentwaters reported lights and a craft; documented in the UK MoD UFO files released by the National Archives.

cases.title: Belgian UFO wave (F-16 pursuit)
cases.description: During the 1989–90 Belgian wave, the Belgian Air Force scrambled F-16s after radar contacts on the night of 30–31 March 1990; the case was studied openly with the military.

cases.title: Manises incident
cases.description: A passenger jet diverted to Manises after lights paced it; the Spanish Air Force scrambled a Mirage F1. Spain later declassified its military UFO (OVNI) archives.

cases.title: Chilean Navy helicopter IR case
cases.description: A Chilean Navy helicopter filmed an unidentified object on IR; Chile's official UAP committee CEFAA studied it for two years before releasing the footage in 2017.

cases.title: Campeche FLIR case
cases.description: A Mexican Air Force surveillance aircraft recorded multiple objects on FLIR during an anti-drug patrol; released publicly by the Mexican military.

cases.agency: Imperial Iranian Air Force (US DIA report)
cases.title: Tehran F-4 incident
cases.description: Two Imperial Iranian Air Force F-4 Phantoms scrambled on an object over Tehran and reported instrument/weapons failures; documented in a now-declassified US Defense Intelligence Agency report.

cases.title: Falcon Lake incident
cases.description: Stefan Michalak reported a landed craft and sustained burns; investigated by the RCMP and Canadian government — one of Canada's best-documented cases.

cases.title: Shag Harbour incident
cases.description: An object reportedly crashed into the water off Shag Harbour; the RCMP and Royal Canadian Navy mounted an official search — documented in Canadian government files.

cases.title: Kaikoura lights
cases.description: Lights filmed from an Argosy aircraft off Kaikoura and tracked on radar; investigated by the RNZAF, with files later released by the NZ government.

cases.title: Voronezh sighting
cases.description: The Soviet state news agency TASS officially reported a landing and sightings in a Voronezh park in 1989 — a rare official Soviet UAP acknowledgement.

cases.title: CRIDOVNI official commission
cases.description: Uruguay's Air Force has run an official UAP investigation commission (CRIDOVNI) since 1979, one of the world's longest-running government UAP bodies, holding hundreds of investigated cases.
